H100_80GB_SXM.source_notes: NVIDIA NIM perf docs; AMD ROCm cross-publication MI300-074; MLPerf v4.1

H200_141GB_SXM.source_notes: MLPerf v5.1 Krai/Dell SGLang 31391 / vLLM 30893 on 8-GPU; CoreWeave MLPerf v5.0 blog

B200_180GB_SXM6_FP8.source_notes: ESTIMATED: halved from Lambda MLPerf v5.1 FP4 result (102725 tok/s on 8 B200, Llama 2 70B Offline). No public B200 FP8 70B benchmark as of 2026-04-30. VERIFY BEFORE COMMITTING.

B200_180GB_SXM6_FP4.source_notes: Lambda MLPerf v5.1 (8x B200 Llama 2 70B Offline = 102725 tok/s). FP4 is high-water for B200; FP8 is more typical for production inference.

MI300X_192GB.confidence_notes: High confidence on range, medium on point estimate
MI300X_192GB.source_notes: AMD ROCm blog MI300-074 Llama 3.1 70B FP8 vLLM; arXiv 2510.27583 (independent academic) shows real-world 37–66% of H100/H200

methodology_notes.sustained_pct_of_peak: Production sustained throughput is typically ~70% of peak aggregate, accounting for tail latency, batch efficiency, and SLO headroom.
methodology_notes.ttft_interpretation: p99 latency targets are interpreted as TTFT for a 1024-token prompt. End-to-end <500ms for 256-token output is infeasible at meaningful concurrency for 70B models on any current accelerator.
